---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

<style>
.dashboard-page{
  max-width:1000px;
  margin:0 auto;
}

.back-btn{
  display:inline-block;
  margin-bottom:1.4rem;
  padding:.55rem 1rem;
  border-radius:999px;
  background:#f3f4f6;
  color:#222;
  text-decoration:none;
  font-weight:600;
  border:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
}

.back-btn:hover{
  background:#e9ecef;
}

.dashboard-frame{
  border-radius:18px;
  overflow:hidden;
  box-shadow:0 10px 28px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
}

.dashboard-note{
  margin:1rem 0;
  padding:0.85rem 1rem;
  border-left:4px solid #2457a6;
  background:#f8f9fb;
  border-radius:8px;
  color:#555;
}
</style>

::: {.dashboard-page}

[← Back to Projects](content.html#cartography-page){.back-btn}

## Environmental Monitoring Dashboard

**Course:** Project Proposal Development and Proof of Concept (FCOR 599)  
*Group project with four MGEM colleagues*

**Role:** Dashboard development and GIS visualization  

**Team Members**

- Nathan Mithal  
- Kate Van den Berg  
- Muhamad Nafi Andriansyah  
- Nicole Graham  


This ArcGIS Dashboard was developed as part of a simulated citizen science exercise conducted in the UBC MGEM classroom, focused on monitoring birds, mammals, and amphibians. The project was designed to explore the opportunities and challenges associated with citizen-generated environmental data, including issues of data reliability, spatial coverage, and observer bias.


### Interactive Dashboard

::: {.dashboard-note}
**Note:** If prompted to sign in to ArcGIS Online, click **Cancel** to view the dashboard.
:::

::: {.dashboard-frame}
<iframe
  src="https://www.arcgis.com/apps/dashboards/1d640b2ee0d1471d9ffe2e9b155568b6"
  width="100%"
  height="650"
  frameborder="0">
</iframe>
:::

[Open dashboard full screen →](https://www.arcgis.com/apps/dashboards/1d640b2ee0d1471d9ffe2e9b155568b6){target="_blank"}

:::
